mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 equities and generating positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 45% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings contraction, causing equity risk premiums to rise and S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above the 6.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p less than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term momentum in equity markets over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases are followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of observations during documented recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation remains elevated above 230 while the federal funds rate stays below 1.0 percent, negative real interest rates trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and yielding negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL exceeds 230 while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0
failure_condition: The joint condition of high CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, with Rank IC below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirm a technical recession, which forces corporate earnings downgrades and risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 shows negative growth for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of confirmed recessionary quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls indicates synchronized supply-side and labor market strength, which reduces recession probabilities and drives positive short-term equity momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 5-day horizons with a p-value less than 0.05.
